message:
feat(fcp-cli): add hot primitive benchmarks and RaptorQ implementation

Expand the FCP benchmark suite with critical path performance measurements.

## New Benchmark Command: `fcp bench primitives`

Microbenchmarks for hot primitives in the connector execution path:
- object-id: ObjectId creation and hashing performance
- capability-verify: Ed25519 signature verification for capability tokens
- session-mac: BLAKE3 keyed MAC for frame authentication
- fcps-frame: FCPS wire protocol frame construction

These primitives are exercised millions of times in production, so
sub-microsecond performance is critical.

Usage:
  fcp bench primitives --target=all
  fcp bench primitives --target=capability-verify

## RaptorQ Benchmark Implementation

Replaced placeholder with actual RaptorQ fountain code benchmarks:
- Configurable payload sizes (1kb, 1mb, 10mb, 100mb)
- Measures encoding throughput and symbol overhead
- Uses fcp-raptorq crate for production-accurate measurements

Usage:
  fcp bench raptorq --size=1mb

## Dependencies

Added to Cargo.toml:
- fcp-core: For ObjectId and core types
- fcp-crypto: For Ed25519 and BLAKE3 MAC
- fcp-raptorq: For fountain code benchmarks
- ed25519-dalek: For key generation in benchmarks

## Integration

`fcp bench all` now includes:
- All CBOR canonical serialization benchmarks
- All hot primitive microbenchmarks
- Default 1MB RaptorQ benchmark

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
